description: Unable to proceed until a named obstacle moves: a missing material, an unmade decision, a door not yet opened. Saying a task is blocked invites the question of what stands in the way; it assigns no blame and does not promise that clearing the named obstacle will be enough.
sound_symbolism: Dental 'no' enters a rounded chamber with a low nasal resonance. Stressed 'ra' breaks that enclosure with a rolling tongue and broad 'a'. Final 'ku' closes firmly at the velum and drops into deep 'u'. The contour opens only in the middle before its last hard contact.
grammatical_notes: An adjective: 'lao muralo to ma shua ha riporu noraku nai.' (Because the material did not come, this task is blocked.) Name the obstacle and evidence rather than using the status as a complete explanation. Distinct from 'hasoru' (pending), 'thonuki' (technical failure), 'naweri' (refuse), and 'te' (cease).

pillars.solarpunk-values: Visible blockers help communities address missing materials, access, care, information, and dependencies instead of hiding them behind individual productivity judgments.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: A non-blaming status word separates inability to proceed from laziness, disobedience, incompetence, or fault.

semantic_domains.activity: a work state unable to proceed under current conditions
